top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Shielded Token Operations: Mint, Transfer & Burn with Test Suite
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Walk through the complete shielded token lifecycle in Compact: minting, transferring, and burning, with compilable code and a full test suite. No UI required, this is focused on the contract and testing layer. Covers: - Minting with `mintShieldedToken` and `evolveNonce` - Transferring with `sendShielded` and change management via `ShieldedSendResult` - Burning via `sendImmediateShielded` to `shieldedBurnAddress()` - The Merkle tree constraint (freshly m

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Replay Attack Prevention in Compact: Nonces, Nullifiers & Domain Separation
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Explain three replay prevention mechanisms: (1) Counter-based nonces that increment per operation, (2) Set-based nullifiers derived from `persistentCommit(secret, context)`, (3) Domain separation tags in hash functions to prevent cross-circuit replay. Show working examples of each and when to use which pattern. ## Target Audience Developers ## Deliverables 1. **Written tutorial (2,500-3,500 words)** covering: - Counter-based nonces that increment per op

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Midnight vs Other Privacy Chains: Architecture Comparison for Developers
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Compare Midnight's architecture (Compact + ZK circuits + Zswap + dual ledger) with other privacy-focused chains (Aztec's Noir + UTXO, Aleo's Leo + records, Mina's o1js + recursive SNARKs, Zcash's Sapling). Focus on developer experience: language design, state model, privacy model. An honest technical comparison, not marketing. ## Target Audience Developers and Web3 Enthusiasts ## Deliverables 1. **Written tutorial (2,000-3,000 words)** covering: - Midni

top_opportunities.title: [Bounty #71 Med tier: 15 RTC] PR #7169 - fix(attest): validate /attest/challenge body to close DoS surface (Issue #7168)
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty #71 claim for PR #7169 (Medium tier, requesting 15 RTC) — Issue #7168 ### Status: MERGED 2026-06-14T18:40:00Z **PR:** https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain/pull/7169 **Merge commit:** `f562fb5a3f45` **Issue:** https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain/issues/7168 ### What this PR fixes `POST /attest/challenge` previously ignored the JSON body entirely and returned HTTP 200 with a valid 5-minute nonce for ANY input shape — empty body, empty dict, integer, list, null, or wildcard miner (`*`)

top_opportunities.title: Add `GitHubIssuePreviewCard` live data fetch from GitHub API
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Problem `GitHubIssuePreviewCard` renders a GitHub issue preview but may be using static data or mocked content instead of fetching live from the GitHub REST API. ## Scope Wire the card to fetch issue metadata (title, labels, state, open date) from `https://api.github.com/repos/:owner/:repo/issues/:number` on mount. ## Acceptance criteria - Card fetches live issue data on mount using the `repo` and `issueNumber` from the bounty - Loading skeleton shown during fetch - Error state shown with lin

top_opportunities.title: [Bounty: 300 beenz] [Feature] [Crypto] Kubernetes for Dogs (on the blockchain)
top_opportunities.body_preview: For too long our canine companions have been unjustly deprived of the opportunity to agilely nete kubers. Without access to hyperfungible IoT fog-computing, they will forever be left behind in the stone age. Imagine being able to deploy Fido his very own cloud-native AJAX single-page REST API, all from the touchscreen of your Juicero. The industry must be disrupted with blockchain-enabled ultrascale agentic quantum hypervisors for deploying serverless femptoservices to the metaverse. For dogs.

top_opportunities.title: Feedback from production integration (LENITNES — Hedera AI Bounty)
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Context We've been running `hedera-agent-kit@^3.8.2` (the v3 line) in production for ~3 weeks as the on-chain anchoring layer for [**LENITNES**](https://lenitnes.persidian.com) — an autonomous AI trading-intelligence agent that anchors its first-person reasoning to Hedera HCS for every signal it scores via the kit's `coreConsensusPlugin` tools.
